title: Wrong behaviour of VL_STREAML_FAST_QQI when lbits=64
body:
The Intel compiler seems to behave differently from GCC on shifting up a QData by 64 bits (which creates an overflow). For example, for Intel compiler (~0ULL)<<64 and ~((~0ULL)<<64) are both 0.

This creates an issue in VL_STREAML_FAST_QQI which always ends up returning 0 when lbits=64.

A fix for this is to change this line in VL_STREAML_FAST_QQI function definition in verilated_funcs.h:
```
        const QData msbMask = lbitsFloor == VL_MASK_Q(lbitsRem) << lbitsFloor;
```
to:
```
        const QData msbMask = lbitsFloor == 64 ? 0ULL : VL_MASK_Q(lbitsRem) << lbitsFloor;
```
